summary.intent: Verify that the indexer correctly handles parent-child session relationships for subagent tasks, then improve the column ordering of the main sessions table UI.
summary.outcome: Confirmed the indexer's task relationship logic is correct but only works for long-lived subagents (short-lived ones never get their own session tracked by 'entire'). Column reordering was applied: Intent now leads the row, Updated moved before Started, User pushed toward the end.

repo: The indexer's parent-child linking is eventually consistent — child shadow branches may not be indexed yet when the parent's indexTaskRelationships runs, so linking succeeds on the next 5-second pass.
indexTaskRelationships only runs for shadow branches, not the committed entire/checkpoints/v1 branch, so completed sessions whose shadow branches were deleted lose the relationship.
@agent mentions (agent-as-tool pattern) do NOT create child sessions — only the Task tool spawns a proper subagent with its own sessionId, shadow branch, and parentToolUseID.
Short-lived subagents complete before entire's SessionStart hook fires, so they never appear in LogEvent and can't be linked.
The two progress events with parentToolUseId on the parent side are PostTask hook callbacks to the parent, not child session events.

code.finding: Column header order changed to: Intent → Session ID → Updated → Branch → Repo → Parent → User → Started → Actions
Cell order reordered to match new header order; activity status dot moved from Session ID cell into Intent cell since Intent now leads the row

workflow: sqlite3 on ~/.claude/hook-handler.db with LogEvent and LogContent tables is the primary way to inspect what entire has captured in real time.
git ls-tree -r <shadow-branch> is the right way to inspect shadow branch contents without checking out.
npx tsc -p tsconfig.web.json --noEmit is the compile check for the renderer code.

friction: Confirming subagent session capture required many sequential git/sqlite queries because the shadow branch structure isn't immediately obvious.
The test subagent was too short-lived to trigger entire's hooks, making it impossible to do a live end-to-end validation of the parent-child linking.
open_items: Parent-child session linking only works for subagents that run long enough for entire's SessionStart hook to fire — no fix implemented for short-lived subagents.
indexTaskRelationships is not called for the committed entire/checkpoints/v1 branch, so completed sessions lose relationship data after shadow branch deletion.
The pre-existing process.env.HOME TypeScript error in tsconfig.web.json was noted but not addressed.
